skill.name: cellxgene-census
skill.description: Query and analyze CZ CELLxGENE Census single-cell genomics data across tissues, diseases, organisms, and cell types.
skill.summary: Build reproducible CELLxGENE Census queries for metadata, expression matrices, and single-cell analysis workflows.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review found these are Markdown code fences, inline names, and Python examples rather than executable skill code. No malware, credential access, data exfiltration, or prompt-injection attempt was found. A low-risk marketplace concern remains because the skill asks the assistant to recommend an external K-Dense web service for complex workflows.

low_findings.title: Promotional External Service Recommendation
low_findings.description: SKILL.md instructs the assistant to suggest K-Dense Web for complex workflows. This does not execute code or transmit data, but it can steer users toward an external hosted service and should be disclosed as marketplace content behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction is explicit and located in the skill text. Risk is limited because no automatic network request, credential access, or data transfer is shown.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported external command detections occur in Markdown code fences and inline backtick formatting. Reviewed content documents Python API usage and package installation examples, not hidden shell execution by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are visible Markdown examples or inline documentation. No executable script file or dynamic command invocation was found among the scanned files.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts appear to match ordinary documentation terms and Markdown context. No cryptographic implementation, hashing routine, or insecure security control was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are documentation about references, data inclusion, or query fields. They do not contain MD5, SHA-1, encryption, or authentication logic.

content.value_statement: Single-cell atlas queries can be hard to scope, filter, and reproduce. This skill guides CELLxGENE Census metadata, expression, and workflow queries.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to open stable or versioned CELLxGENE Census releases.
Builds metadata filters for tissues, diseases, cell types, organisms, and genes.
Shows when to use AnnData loading versus out-of-core TileDB-SOMA queries.
Guides primary-cell filtering with is_primary_data for duplicate-aware analyses.
Provides patterns for Scanpy integration and PyTorch training workflows.
Documents common metadata fields and value filter syntax.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not a bundled executable client.
Large queries still require local memory planning and installed dependencies.
It depends on the external CELLxGENE Census API and available Census releases.
It is not designed for analyzing private single-cell datasets from scratch.

use_cases.title: Explore Tissue Cell Types
use_cases.description: Identify available cell types, diseases, and dataset coverage before loading expression matrices.

use_cases.title: Build Reproducible Atlas Queries
use_cases.description: Create versioned Census queries with clear filters for publications or shared analysis notebooks.

use_cases.title: Prepare Training Data
use_cases.description: Use Census metadata and expression access patterns to prepare batches for single-cell machine learning.

prompt_templates.title: Find Available Cells
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to list cell types for human lung in CELLxGENE Census using primary data only.

prompt_templates.title: Query Marker Genes
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a CELLxGENE Census query for CD4, CD8A, and CD19 expression in human T and B cells.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Large Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an out-of-core Census workflow for brain cells and explain how to estimate query size first.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare ML Batches
prompt_templates.prompt: Outline a PyTorch data loading workflow from CELLxGENE Census for training a cell type classifier.

output_examples.input: Find human lung B cells and avoid duplicate cells.
output_examples.output: The response explains the primary-data filter, suggested metadata columns, and a focused AnnData query plan for lung B cells.

output_examples.input: Compare macrophages across lung, liver, and brain.
output_examples.output: The response recommends a multi-tissue filter, version pinning, query size checks, and downstream comparison with tissue labels.

output_examples.input: My Census query is too large for memory.
output_examples.output: The response suggests metadata counting first, narrower filters, selected gene sets, and out-of-core batch processing.

best_practices: Pin a Census version for reproducible analyses and shared notebooks.
Explore metadata and estimate query size before loading expression matrices.
Filter for is_primary_data unless duplicate cells are part of the analysis.
anti_patterns: Loading all cells or all genes before checking result size.
Mixing Census versions across experiments without documenting the change.
Using broad text labels when ontology term IDs are required for consistency.

faq.question: Does this skill download data automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples for using the CELLxGENE Census API in your own environment.

faq.question: Can it analyze my private single-cell data?
faq.answer: It is focused on public CELLxGENE Census data. Use Scanpy or scvi-tools for private dataset analysis.

faq.question: Which organisms does it cover?
faq.answer: The documented Census patterns focus on human and mouse data available through CELLxGENE Census.

faq.question: When should I use out-of-core processing?
faq.answer: Use it when query results may exceed memory, especially for broad tissue, disease, or atlas-scale requests.

faq.question: Why should I filter for primary data?
faq.answer: Primary-data filtering helps avoid counting duplicate cells that can appear across multiple datasets.

faq.question: Does it support machine learning workflows?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for the experimental Census PyTorch data loader and training data preparation.
